text.title: Design Critique Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design Critique. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every feedback item references the design goal or a heuristic; bare 'I would do it differently' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Goal-anchored feedback turns opinion into decision input.

rule.statement: Designer presents context, goal, and specific feedback ask; critique without an ask becomes a free-for-all.
rule.rationale: Without a designer-set ask, the room defaults to taste.

rule.statement: Session is time-boxed (45 to 90 minutes) with structure: context, walk-through, feedback per dimension, decisions, next actions.
rule.rationale: Unstructured sessions blow time and surface low-signal points.

rule.statement: Decisions and follow-up actions are logged in a shared doc within 24 hours.
rule.rationale: Unlogged decisions are re-argued the next sprint.

rule.statement: Critique is not a sign-off; approvals follow a separate decision review.
rule.rationale: Conflating critique and approval causes designers to avoid honest critique.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-critique.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
